entities.name: śphoṭa
entities.cite: sounds stand to the eternal word as the cow stands to cowhood

entities.name: śabda
entities.cite: sounds stand to the eternal word as the cow stands to cowhood

entities.name: āśraya
entities.cite: universals are manifested by their asrayas, the substrates in which they inhere

entities.name: gatvam
entities.cite: cowhood becomes apprehensible exactly where a cow stands

entities.name: gau
entities.cite: cowhood becomes apprehensible exactly where a cow stands

entities.name: anitya
entities.cite: manifestation is at home only among anitya things

axioms.name: vyakti
axioms.cite: universals, nitya by everyone's admission, are manifested (vyaktir isyate) by their asrayas, the substrates in which they inhere

axioms.name: nitya
axioms.cite: sounds stand to the eternal word as the cow stands to cowhood

denials.name: anitya
denials.cite: how can the *eternal* be manifested without being dragged into time?

claims.name: vyakti
claims.cite: manifestation of the eternal universals by their substrates is likewise accepted

rejected_readings.label: eternal_temporalized_sphota
rejected_readings.rendering: To manifest the eternal word (sphota) through sounds would drag it into time and make it transient.
rejected_readings.why: Asserts that manifestation of eternal things necessarily makes them temporal. The verse refutes this through the precedent of eternal universals: cowhood is manifested whenever a cow appears, yet cowhood neither arrives nor departs. The manifestation relation does not transmit transience to what is manifested.

rejected_readings.label: eternal_temporalized_jati
rejected_readings.rendering: Manifestation of eternal universals would render them subject to temporal change, arriving and departing with their substrates.
rejected_readings.why: Asserts that manifestation of eternals makes them transient. The commentary directly refutes this: cowhood is manifested by the cow standing, yet 'cowhood neither arrives nor departs.' The axiom of eternals excludes the anitya property.

rejected_readings.label: substrate_manifests_property
rejected_readings.rendering: The manifestation of universals by substrates shows that universals are mere properties dependent on their substrates, not eternal principles.
rejected_readings.why: Confuses manifestation (occurrence of apprehension) with ontological dependence (sort error: power vs property). The commentary emphasizes that cowhood is eternally real and neither arrives nor departs when the substrate appears or disappears. Manifestation is an epistemic/phenomenal relation, not an ontological one.

notes: The verse uses manifestation (vyakti) of eternals as a dialectical move to dissolve an objection. It does not establish that the word-essence (sphota) IS eternal (that is asserted elsewhere); it removes the incoherence of saying so. The key strain: manifestation is a relation that works across the eternal-transient divide, which challenges simple ontological categorization. The contract encodes this by treating manifestation as a relation between power (jati) and manifestation (asraya), parallel to the brahman-sakti-jagat structure in 1.1-1.2.
